verdict: The AI displacement risk for chefs is moderate, with some tasks being automated but human judgment and creativity still essential.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like checking the quality of raw or cooked food products (Task 2), analyzing recipes to assign prices to menu items (Task 7), and preparing and cooking foods of all types (Task 9) are heavily reliant on routine cognition, which is already being automated by AI.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like determining how food should be presented and creating decorative food displays (Task 6) require creative judgment, which is still unique to humans.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Summary and definition of the chef profession

adjacent_careers.name: Restaurant Manager
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both chefs and restaurant managers are responsible for overseeing kitchen operations, but restaurant managers have more administrative tasks and less hands-on cooking responsibilities.

adjacent_careers.name: Food Critic
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Food critics rely on their creative judgment to evaluate the quality of food, making them a good adjacent career for chefs.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered kitchen management system launched
recent_developments.impact: This system can automate some tasks like menu planning and inventory management, but human chefs will still be needed to make creative decisions.

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.
